findings.description: Severe structural dislocation with duplicated headers and misplaced footers.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, after the initial logo and top-right header block, there is a massive blank gap, followed by the document footer rendered inline, then a duplicated header block, all occurring before the main document title 'PROCLAMATION'.

findings.description: Footer text interrupts the main reading order.
findings.evidence: The footer text ('State of North Carolina...') is rendered inline as regular body text between list item VI.C and list item D, breaking the continuity of the text flow.

findings.description: Extraneous header and footer text rendered inline.
findings.evidence: Footer text is injected as standard text at the very top of the page before the page header, and the footer block is duplicated at the bottom of the page.

findings.description: Pagination artifacts (page headers and footers) are embedded directly within the ordered lists, interrupting the logical flow and semantic structure of the content.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, between list items VI.C and VI.D, the text 'State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries... Proclamation PT-2-2024 Pg. 2' is inserted inside the <li> tag for VI.C. A similar interruption occurs between IX.E and X.
